content.full_text:
February 25, 1983

Mr. David Bane  
3434 Grove  
Lemon Grove, CA 92045

Re: Your Request For Advice,  
Our Advice No. A-83-048

Dear Mr. Bane:

Thank you for your request for advice on Government Code Section 84308* (AB 1040). You are the campaign treasurer for a mayoral candidate, Maureen O'Connor, who currently is a Board member for the San Diego Port Authority. As a Board Member, Ms. O'Connor may be confronted with decisions on permits and other entitlements for use which directly affect companies from whom she received contributions. In order for Ms. O'Connor to determine whether a contribution of $250 or more has been received from a company, you have asked the following questions:

1. If a corporation and a corporate officer give separate contributions, must the amounts be aggregated?

2. If a corporation and a major shareholder give separate contributions, must the amounts be aggregated?

If the contributing company is a closely-held corporation and the corporate officer or shareholder is a major stockholder in the company, the contributions given by each are aggregated. In any other situation, regardless

*Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

of the size of the corporation, the contributions are not aggregated, unless the officer or shareholder is reimbursed for the contributions by the company.

I hope this clarifies this matter for you. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:km
